hook: Luca reaches Ray's office door and sees through the glass panel that Ray is holding the signed document over an open flame — he is seconds away from destroying it.
goal: Luca must stop Ray from destroying the document and return the metal box to the Cole family's descendants.

start_state.location: Walking the street toward Ray Brennan's office on Cale Street, Harden
items: handwritten note (Cole family left Harden July 1974; E. Ward)
paper with Ray Brennan's Cale Street office address
knowledge: Edward Ward borrowed a large sum from the Cole family and signed a document promising repayment
Edward Ward used his connections to force the Cole family out of Harden in 1974 to escape the debt
The metal box holds the signed document and part of the original money
Ray Brennan stole the box from Luca's flat
Ray Brennan visited Agnes Ward two days ago and told her the situation was under control
Agnes Ward kept silent for two years after learning the truth
suspicions: Ray intends to destroy the document inside the metal box before Luca can use it as proof

continuity_rules: Must open after Luca has already left Birch Street — do not revisit or summarise the Agnes Ward conversation
Must not re-explain the content of Agnes's confession — the reader already knows it
Ray Brennan currently holds the metal box — his possession of it must be visible at the start of the confrontation
The video camera with tape must be used as Luca's active leverage — it is the item that shifts power in the confrontation
Do not contradict: Ray stole the box, Agnes has no key items, Luca has the camera
key_events: Luca arrives at Ray's office on Cale Street and pauses at the door: through the glass panel he sees Ray sitting at his desk with the metal box open and the signed document in his hands, holding it toward a lit match — he is about to destroy it; this is what the reader has feared since Episode 5 and Luca sees it happening right now; Luca pushes through the door hard before Ray can act. [Dramatic irony payoff: reader knew Ray intended to destroy the document; the timing is now razor-thin]

Ray is startled; the match goes out; he closes his hand around the document and tells Luca to leave, his voice flat and controlled; Luca says Agnes Ward told him everything; Ray says Luca has no proof and nothing here belongs to him; hard, clipped dialogue — Ray: 'You have nothing.' / Luca: 'I have the tape.' / Ray: 'A tape proves nothing.' / Luca: 'Then why did you take the box from my flat?' — Ray does not answer; the silence is the answer.

Luca sets the video camera on Ray's desk and says clearly: the tape is evidence of the box being hidden, and he will carry it to the police in the next ten minutes unless Ray gives him the box right now; Ray stares at the camera — he cannot destroy what Luca is holding; he can burn the document, but the tape will still exist and Luca's testimony will still exist; a police search of this office would find everything; he is trapped and he knows it.

Ray pushes the metal box across the desk without a word — he admits, in that single gesture, that it is over; then, in a quiet and bitter voice, he tells Luca something Luca did not know: he has always had the Cole granddaughter's address, in the next town, because for fifty years he has been watching in case she ever came looking; he slides a piece of paper toward Luca with her name and address on it and says, 'Give it to her. Then it is finished.' This is the major reveal: Ray's cover-up was deliberate and long-running, not merely passive.

Luca travels to the next town and finds the Cole granddaughter at her home; he gives her the metal box without a long explanation — he says only that it belonged to her family and was kept from them; she opens the box, reads the signed document, and holds it in both hands, saying nothing; Luca leaves; the secret that was sealed under the floorboards fifty years ago is finally out; back in Harden, Ray Brennan and Agnes Ward will face the consequences of what they knew.
major_reveal: Ray Brennan has always known where the Cole family's granddaughter lives and has been monitoring her location for fifty years — his cover-up was active and deliberate, not merely the result of staying quiet.
threads_advanced: The metal box is recovered from Ray Brennan and delivered to its rightful owner
Ray Brennan's full role — active and long-running — is finally exposed
The Cole family's story reaches a direct resolution through the granddaughter
Agnes Ward's silence and Ray's protection of the secret both meet their consequences
threads_resolved: What the metal box contains and who it belongs to
Who stole the box from Luca's flat and why
Why the Cole family left Harden in 1974 and never returned
Who in Harden has been protecting the secret and how deliberately
Whether the Cole family will ever learn the truth
tone_guardrails: Ray's defeat must be quiet and controlled — he is a cautious man; he does not shout or threaten physically
The Cole granddaughter scene must be brief and restrained — one or two sentences of dialogue at most, no emotional speech
The ending must feel earned and still, not triumphant — a wrong made right, not a celebration
No new facts or complications after Ray's reveal in event 4 — the last beat is pure resolution
Consequences for Ray and Agnes are implied in a single closing sentence — do not detail legal outcomes
forbidden_moves: Do not re-explain Agnes's confession — reference it only in a single short line if needed
Do not introduce any new mystery, question, or complication
Do not have Ray physically threaten or attack Luca — the confrontation is verbal and calculated
Do not make the Cole granddaughter a developed character — she is the recipient of the resolution, not a new presence
Do not specify legal punishments for Ray or Agnes — leave consequences concrete but brief
ending_hook: Luca walks away from the Cole granddaughter's door; for the first time since he found the camera in that dusty back room, he does not need to know anything more — the answer is in her hands.
